signal_title: FTC's Social Media and Video Streaming Services Report
risk_rationale: The FTC report highlights concerns about content moderation and its impact on children and teens, aligning with existing TikTok policies but indicating potential areas for enhancement.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: CSAM detection using PhotoDNA + proprietary classifiers
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Enhanced content moderation practices for child safety
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The FTC report suggests a need for more robust content moderation practices to protect children and teens.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: No mature content surfaced to minors
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Enhanced protection against harmful content for minors
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The report implies a need for stricter controls on content exposure to minors.

remediations.title: Enhance Content Moderation for Child Safety
remediations.description: Implement additional content moderation measures to better protect children and teens from harmful content.
acceptance_criteria: Improved detection accuracy for harmful content
Reduced exposure of mature content to minors

remediations.title: Strengthen Content Filtering for Minors
remediations.description: Develop more stringent content filtering mechanisms to ensure no mature content is accessible to minors.
acceptance_criteria: No mature content accessible to accounts flagged as minors
Regular audits of content filtering effectiveness

business_impact: Enhancing content moderation and filtering for minors could increase user trust and compliance with regulatory expectations, potentially reducing legal risks.
recommended_actions: Conduct a detailed review of current content moderation practices
Develop a roadmap for implementing enhanced content filtering
Engage with FTC to clarify expectations and demonstrate compliance efforts
